skills.name: agent-orchestration
skills.description: Coordinate parallel sub-agents and worktrees for complex multi-part tasks. Use when a task is large enough to split across multiple agents, when parallel git worktrees are needed, or when orchestrating multiple coding agents simultaneously. Triggers: "run this in parallel", "split this across agents", "use multiple worktrees", "orchestrate sub-agents". Do NOT use for single-agent tasks.

skills.name: bug-fix-workflow
skills.description: Structured bug fix process. Use when asked to fix a bug, investigate an error, address a failing test, or resolve a broken flow. Steps: reproduce → isolate → fix → test → document. Use for any "something is broken" request. Do NOT use for feature additions, refactors, code reviews, or general quality audits — requires a specific confirmed bug to fix.

skills.name: code-review-quality
skills.description: Code review against WJP quality bar. Use when asked to review code, audit a codebase, check code quality, or evaluate a PR/diff. Produces prioritized findings with severity ratings. Use for any "review this", "what's wrong with this", or "audit this code" request. Do NOT use for shipping, bug fixing, or website/site/product page audits.

skills.name: ship-flow
skills.description: End-to-end shipping workflow for TypeScript/JavaScript projects. Use when asked to ship, deploy, push, release, or publish code changes. Covers TypeScript type-check → build → commit → push → deploy verify → smoke test. Use for any "ship this" or "get this live" request. Do NOT use for pure code review without shipping.

skills.name: site-audit
skills.description: Audit live websites and products for functional bugs, UX issues, developer docs gaps, and AI slop in copy. Use when asked to audit, review, or QA a website, web app, product page, or documentation site. Triggers include: "audit this site", "review these pages", "QA the docs", "check for slop", "UX review", "find what's broken", "check all the links", "review the copy". Supports four audit types — functional, UX, docs, copy/slop — or any combination. Do NOT use for code/PR reviews or bug fixes in a codebase.
